verdict_summary: All five iteration-1 BA-QA objections (OBJ-1 through OBJ-5) are resolved in the iteration-2 revised ticket. All seven iteration-2 codex F1-F7 refinements are correctly applied. Spot-check of F1 (fd 9 inside redirected brace-group) and F6 (AC6 'what was removed' logging) both pass. No new in_scope_real_bug contradictions found. Iteration-1 objections #1-#6 individually remain resolved (per prior QA's VERIFY-1 through VERIFY-8 evidence). The ticket is implementable by a competent dev without further BA revision.

verifications.description: Spot-check iteration-1 objections #1-#6 (happy-attachments line 86, AC1 vs M1, AC5 DIRECTLY, AC4 dry-run channel, AC3 both-mounts, settings.json anchor-based citations) remain resolved
evidence: ticket Evidence lines 56-69: TWO EXCLUSION-FIRST conflicts (line 61 chrome-debug-profile + line 86 happy-attachments) both listed; complete grep result documented
ticket M3 lines 178-179 + AC4 lines 510-514: BOTH literal removals mandated; both paths remain in EXCLUDE_PRUNE
ticket AC1 lines 358-381: emits exactly TWO compact lines per mount with WARN per-line; concrete example outputs included for both-≤75% and one-over-threshold cases
ticket M1 lines 148-151: mirrors AC1 — TWO lines, WARN per-line, no single combined banner-flag form
ticket AC5 lines 516-527: NO 'DIRECTLY' wording; uses 'find /tmp/claude-0 -mindepth 2 -type f -name *.output -mtime +1' (recursive depth-≥2 form)
ticket AC4 lines 479-486: explicit conditional exec-redirect on DRY_RUN=0; tee-to-stdout-AND-log shape mandated; stdout primary, log also acceptable
ticket AC3 lines 397-418: separate top-5 block per pressured mount; concrete example shows BOTH /tmp and /dev/shm blocks when both over threshold
ticket Evidence + Scope sections: anchor-based language consistently used for settings.json (JSON keys, not absolute line numbers); revision-time line numbers labeled as advisory only

verifications.description: M2 flock subshell: parent shell receives usable signal; brace-group-inside-command-substitution is canonical; trailing-redirection rejected
evidence: ticket M2 Tech Hint lines 642-666: canonical shape uses `decision=$( { flock -x 9 || { printf 'skip'; exit 0; }; ...; } 9> "$lock_file" )` — fd 9 opened INSIDE command substitution via redirected brace-group
ticket M2 Tech Hint line 667: parent reads decision via command substitution and applies `[ "$decision" = "emit" ] || exit 0` — parent shell short-circuits when decision == 'skip'
ticket M2 Tech Hint lines 676-677: trailing-redirection form `decision=$( ... ) 9> "$lock_file"` is EXPLICITLY REJECTED with codex F1 citation
ticket AC3 lines 428-436: same brace-group shape mandated in AC; trailing-redirection form REJECTED in AC text as well

ticket Edge Cases lines 278-285: 'a bare exit 0 inside the ( ... ) 9>lockfile subshell only exits the subshell' — OBJ-1 rationale stated explicitly; parent-shell short-circuit via command substitution is the mandated path

Grep search confirms ONLY TWO occurrences of the trailing-redirection phrasing — both at lines 432 and 677, both in REJECTED context with codex F1 citation. No surviving acceptable use of the trailing-redirection form anywhere in the ticket.

Bash semantics claim (fd 9 is visible to flock inside the brace-group when redirection is on the OUTER `$()`) accepted per BA's empirical assertion as instructed by prompt — claim is internally consistent with the canonical bash idiom for command-substitution-with-fd-redirection

verifications.description: stdout-only purge — canonical phrase is 'tee to BOTH stdout AND log'; no surviving 'stdout-only / dev choice / either-or' alternative
evidence: Grep search: TWO occurrences of 'stdout-only' in the ticket — both at lines 341 and 720, both inside REJECTED-context phrasing ('any other dry-run output shape ... is explicitly REJECTED per OBJ-2' at line 341; 'stdout-only-without-log, log-only, alternating, or "dev choice" is REJECTED' at line 720)
Grep search: TWO occurrences of 'dev choice' / 'dev-choice' — both at lines 341 and 720, both in REJECTED-context lists

ticket Edge Cases lines 336-345: canonical single-position is 'dry-run MUST tee to BOTH stdout AND log' (line 339-340); 'any other dry-run output shape ... is explicitly REJECTED per OBJ-2: the spec is now single-canonical on this point — tee to BOTH channels for dry-run'

ticket M3 Tech Hint lines 709-723: code block shows `exec > >(tee -a "$LOG") 2>&1` for DRY_RUN=1 branch; 'The tee-to-stdout-AND-log shape above is the SOLE acceptable form for dry-run (per OBJ-2 ...)'
ticket AC4 lines 479-486: stdout is primary required channel; log is also acceptable; the tee-to-stdout-AND-log shape is mandated via the conditional exec-redirect
No occurrence of 'either (a)' / 'either OR' acceptable-alternative phrasing for dry-run output channel anywhere in the ticket

verifications.description: lock primitive — flock is SOLE acceptable primitive; atomic-mkdir is FORBIDDEN
evidence: Grep search: SIX occurrences of 'atomic-`mkdir`' / 'atomic mkdir' — ALL in FORBIDDEN context: lines 158 ('atomic-`mkdir` is forbidden per OBJ-3'), 271 ('Atomic-`mkdir` locks are FORBIDDEN'), 425 ('atomic-`mkdir` is FORBIDDEN per OBJ-3'), 504 ('atomic-`mkdir` directories would never match `-type f`'), 681 ('atomic-`mkdir` alternative has been retracted per OBJ-3'), 761 ('Atomic-`mkdir` locks are FORBIDDEN per OBJ-3')

ticket M2 lines 158-162: MoSCoW M2 mandates flock as SOLE acceptable lock primitive with OBJ-3 rationale ('the resulting lock directories would never be swept by the Layer-2 `-type f` hook-state predicate and would leak indefinitely')
ticket Edge Cases lines 268-285: Counter race + leak section explicitly retracts the mkdir alternative; flock is the SOLE acceptable lock primitive
ticket AC3 lines 425-427: AC mandates `flock` (atomic-`mkdir` is FORBIDDEN per OBJ-3)

ticket M3 Tech Hint lines 681-684 + 755-762: hook-state tier sweep uses preserved `-type f` predicate (per AC7); flock creates regular files matched by `claude-pressure-warn-*` pattern; atomic-mkdir directories would NOT match
No occurrence of 'either flock or mkdir' / 'mkdir alternative is acceptable' / 'mkdir-lock alternative' anywhere in the ticket. The previous OBJ-3 ambiguity is resolved: flock is the SOLE primitive.

verifications.description: AC6 freed-bytes formula widens to include /var/tmp/codex-outputs delta; AC and Tech Hint mutually consistent
evidence: ticket AC6 line 529 (header): 'Cleanup logs report bytes freed (TOTAL across BOTH /tmp AND /var/tmp/codex-outputs)' — header explicitly widens to dual-mount scope

ticket AC6 lines 532-548: formula `freed_total_KB = (after_tmp_KB - before_tmp_KB) + (after_var_KB - before_var_KB)` with negative clamping; numfmt --to=iec-i --suffix=B; rejects /tmp-only formula explicitly ('A formula that measures /tmp only ... does NOT satisfy AC6')

ticket M3 Tech Hint lines 768-789: code block matches AC6 formula exactly — `tmp_freed_kb = after_tmp - before_tmp`, `var_freed_kb = after_var - before_var`, clamp negatives to 0, sum, format via numfmt --to=iec-i --suffix=B. 'A formula that measures /tmp only ... does NOT satisfy AC6 because the /var/tmp/codex-outputs >7d sweep at the end of the script frees additional bytes that the user's total freed intent includes.'
ticket M3 strategy lines 180-186: matches AC6 — 'TOTAL freed-bytes line summed across BOTH the /tmp delta AND the /var/tmp/codex-outputs delta (per OBJ-4)'
ticket AC7 line 589: enumerates '/var/tmp/codex-outputs >7d — UNCHANGED' as one of the FIVE preserved sweep categories — the category that AC6's widened formula now includes

Grep search: nine occurrences of 'var/tmp/codex-outputs' / 'var/tmp' across AC6 header, AC6 body, AC7 enumeration, M3 MoSCoW, M3 Tech Hint, and strategy — all mutually consistent. No surviving /tmp-only formula anywhere.

verifications.description: PATH-shim validation specifies (a) PATH-resolved df/du and (b) distinct session_ids across iterations
evidence: ticket Edge Cases lines 310-322: PATH-shim validation section explicitly mandates 'the M2 hook MUST resolve df and du via PATH lookup — i.e., the script body uses bare df / du invocations'; absolute paths /usr/bin/df / /usr/bin/du / command df / command du are REJECTED

ticket AC3 lines 447-466: AC explicitly states 'the hook MUST resolve df and du via PATH lookup (NOT via absolute paths like /usr/bin/df or /usr/bin/du) — this is REQUIRED for AC3's PATH-shim validation approach (per OBJ-5)'
ticket M2 Tech Hint line 630: 'df MUST be resolved via PATH lookup (bare df invocation — NOT /usr/bin/df) per OBJ-5'
ticket M2 Tech Hint lines 685-692: 'du MUST also be resolved via PATH (bare du — NOT /usr/bin/du) per OBJ-5'

ticket AC3 lines 455-466: test driver MUST use a DISTINCT session_id for each of the 4 test iterations (e.g., ba-qa-iter1, ba-qa-iter2, ba-qa-iter3, ba-qa-iter4); reusing one session_id would saturate the counter and mask shim variation; an additional FIFTH test uses the SAME session_id for rate-limit verification

context JSON development_approach.validation_approach (line 272): specifies '(a) the M2 hook MUST resolve df/du via PATH lookup' AND '(b) Test driver prepends a temp dir to PATH containing executable shims that emulate df/du with synthetic high-usage output' AND '(c) each iteration uses a DISTINCT session_id ... so per-session counters reset'

context JSON development_approach.strategy clause (l) (within the line-262 strategy block): 'M2 hook MUST resolve df/du via PATH lookup (bare df / du — NOT /usr/bin/df / /usr/bin/du) per OBJ-5 so the QA PATH-shim test driver can replace them for synthetic >75% scenarios without risking real tmpfs fill; QA driver MUST use DISTINCT session_id per iteration to avoid counter saturation masking shim variation.'

Both PATH-shim requirement and distinct-session_id requirement appear in BOTH the ticket (for dev) AND the context JSON (for QA validation), so dev cannot miss either constraint and QA cannot fail an implementation that satisfies them

verifications.description: Spot-check codex F1: fd 9 inside `$( { ... } 9>lockfile )`; trailing-redirection form does NOT appear as acceptable shape
evidence: ticket M2 Tech Hint code block at lines 642-666: canonical shape is `decision=$( { flock -x 9 || { ... }; ...; } 9> "$lock_file" )` — fd 9 opened INSIDE command substitution via redirected brace-group
ticket lines 432 and 677: trailing-redirection form `decision=$( ... ) 9> "$lock_file"` is EXPLICITLY REJECTED with codex F1 citation at both sites

ticket lines 642-647: 'fd 9 MUST be opened INSIDE the command substitution via a redirected brace-group (per iteration-2 codex F1) — a trailing 9> "$lock_file" AFTER $( ... ) does not reliably attach fd 9 to commands running inside the command substitution, so flock -x 9 can fail with Bad file descriptor and leave the critical section unsynchronized'
ticket AC3 lines 428-436: AC text mirrors the constraint — brace-group shape required, trailing-redirection form REJECTED

ticket lines 673-676: equivalent shapes (e.g., a redirected subshell `( ... ) 9>"$lock_file"` placed INSIDE `$(...)`) are explicitly allowed as long as fd 9 is opened in the same shell scope where flock -x 9 runs — this clarifies the canonical-shape constraint without admitting the rejected trailing-redirection form
Grep search for trailing-redirection phrasing returns ONLY two hits, both in REJECTED context. No surviving acceptable use of the trailing-redirection form anywhere in the ticket.

verifications.description: Spot-check codex F6: AC6 requires BOTH freed-bytes AND 'what was removed' logging (per-category candidate paths OR removed_count)
evidence: ticket AC6 lines 552-559: 'for real runs (DRY_RUN=0), each sweep category MUST log EITHER the candidate paths selected for deletion (one path per line, before the actual rm) OR a per-category removed-count summary line (e.g., `category=playwright-profiles removed_count=12`). The existing `--- <desc> ---` category header line alone does NOT satisfy the user requirement document's "log what it removed and how much space was freed" mandate (codex F6 raised this gap — AC6 previously asserted only the freed-bytes half). Dev may choose either logging shape per category; whichever shape is chosen must be applied uniformly across all five sweep categories'
context JSON development_approach.files_to_modify (within the line-270 entry): 'per-category "what it removed" logging [codex F6: either candidate-paths-before-rm or per-category removed_count line]'

context JSON codex_consult.findings F6 entry (lines 378-383): finding classified as major, disposition=applied; rationale 'Closes the gap between AC6 and the user requirement doc line 53 ("what it removed and how much space was freed"); AC6 + context.development_approach.files_to_modify + validation_approach all updated.'

AC6 thus requires BOTH the freed-bytes total line AND per-category removed/candidate logging — the two clauses are conjunctive within AC6 (the freed-bytes clause is the first paragraph at lines 530-548; the 'what was removed' clause is the new paragraph at lines 552-559)
User requirement document line 53 ('what it removed and how much space was freed') is now fully covered by AC6's two clauses

objections.claim: M2 flock-in-subshell example contradicts its own parent-shell short-circuit requirement.
objections.ticket_evidence: ticket.md M2 Technical Hint lines 530-535: example is '(flock -x 9; n=$(cat <counter_file> 2>/dev/null || echo 0); [ $n -ge 3 ] && exit 0; echo $((n+1)) > <counter_file>) 9> <counter_file>.lock' — the [ $n -ge 3 ] && exit 0 fires INSIDE the subshell, so the subshell exits with status 0. The very next sentence (line 534-535) says 'Implement the rate-limit short-circuit at parent-shell level (should_warn || exit 0) so a subshell exit 0 cannot leak through and still emit' — but the example code IS exactly the leak-prone shape the text warns against.
objections.problem: A dev who copy-pastes the example verbatim will produce a hook that always prints the warning, because the rate-limit check returns 0 to the parent shell regardless of whether the limit is exhausted. AC3 'AT MOST 3 times per session' is violated.
objections.required_evidence: BA must replace the example with a function returning nonzero when exhausted, e.g.: consume_warn_slot() { { flock -x 9 || return 1; n=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); [[ $n =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || n=0; (( n >= 3 )) && return 1; printf '%s\n' $((n+1)) > "$counter_file"; return 0; } 9>"$counter_file.lock"; }; consume_warn_slot || exit 0. The example MUST exhibit the parent-level short-circuit, not contradict it.

objections.claim: F5 is only PARTIALLY applied; stale 'stdout-only — dev choice' wording remains in Edge Cases section.
objections.ticket_evidence: ticket.md Edge Cases lines 291-296 say 'dev MUST condition this redirect on DRY_RUN=0 (real run: append to log only — current behavior; dry-run: tee to stdout AND log, or stdout-only — dev choice as long as tmp-cleanup.sh --dry-run | grep <pattern> yields candidates)'. ticket.md M3 Technical Hint lines 555-561 say 'The tee-to-stdout-AND-log shape above is the REQUIRED form for dry-run' and 'Plain stdout-only without log tee is NOT acceptable'.
objections.problem: Direct internal contradiction within the same ticket. A dev reading Edge Cases picks stdout-only as 'dev choice'; a dev reading Technical Hints chooses tee-to-BOTH. QA verification mis-fires. This is the exact F5 issue VERIFY-7 was supposed to confirm fully resolved — QA missed the Edge Case stale wording because QA only spot-checked the Technical Hint side.
objections.required_evidence: Delete the 'or stdout-only — dev choice' wording from Edge Cases lines 294-295. Replace with 'dry-run: tee to stdout AND log via exec > >(tee -a $LOG) 2>&1 — the REQUIRED form per Technical Hints M3; stdout-only without log copy is NOT acceptable (codex F5).'

objections.claim: Atomic-mkdir lock cleanup mismatch: M2 alternative produces directories but M3 sweep relies on the existing -type f predicate (AC7 mandates preservation of existing sweep predicates).
objections.ticket_evidence: ticket.md M2 Technical Hint lines 533-535 allow 'OR the mkdir <counter_file>.lock || exit 0 atomic-mkdir alternative followed by read/increment/write/cleanup'. ticket.md M3 lines 593-595 sweep 'claude-pressure-warn-*.lock if the atomic-mkdir lock alternative is chosen'. ticket.md AC7 lines 477-479 say 'the existing FIVE tmp-cleanup sweep categories continue to fire with their current predicate sets' — and the existing hook-state tier uses -type f. mkdir-based locks are DIRECTORIES, which a -type f sweep skips.
objections.problem: If dev chooses the atomic-mkdir alternative (allowed by M2), lock directories accumulate under /tmp forever — the meta-leak the codex iter-1 #7 finding tried to close is NOT closed. The /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-*.lock pattern is in the sweep list but the -type f predicate excludes it.
objections.required_evidence: BA must pick ONE of (a) mandate the flock lock-file implementation only (drop the mkdir alternative); or (b) add a separate >7d top-level /tmp sweep specifically for -type d -name 'claude-pressure-warn-*.lock' using the same EXCLUDE_PRUNE structure, called out explicitly so dev cannot miss it. Either is acceptable; the current spec is implementation-ambiguous.

objections.claim: AC6 'TOTAL freed-bytes summed across all categories' contradicts the Technical Hint which computes only /tmp delta.
objections.ticket_evidence: ticket.md AC6 lines 444-445 say '/var/log/tmp-cleanup.log contains a TOTAL freed-bytes entry summed across all categories'. ticket.md AC7 line 484 explicitly preserves '/var/tmp/codex-outputs >7d — UNCHANGED' as one of the FIVE sweep categories. ticket.md M3 Technical Hint lines 601-604 say 'Compute freed bytes as (after_tmp_KB - before_tmp_KB) * 1024'.
objections.problem: The Technical Hint formula measures only /tmp; the /var/tmp/codex-outputs sweep operates outside /tmp and contributes freed bytes that AC6 mandates be included in the TOTAL. A dev following the Technical Hint would log a freed_total that excludes /var/tmp/codex-outputs and still claim AC6 PASS. AC6 grep would PASS for the wrong reason.
objections.required_evidence: Update Technical Hint line 601-604 to: 'Compute freed bytes as ((after_tmp - before_tmp) + (after_var_tmp - before_var_tmp)) * 1024 where var_tmp samples df -P /var/tmp before and after; clamp negative results to 0; then format with numfmt --to=iec-i --suffix=B'. OR narrow AC6 wording from 'summed across all categories' to 'summed across /tmp categories' if /var/tmp accounting is out of scope. Either fix removes the contradiction.

objections.claim: AC3 PATH-shim validation method is under-specified — hook may use absolute /bin/df / /usr/bin/du and shims won't intercept; counter file reuse across iterations not specified.
objections.ticket_evidence: context.json development_approach.validation_approach line 264 relies on 'prepend a temp dir to PATH containing executable shims that emulate df/du'. ticket.md M2 Technical Hint lines 519-540 do not require the hook to call PATH-resolved df/du. Lines 521-524 say 'invoke the userprompt-tmpfs-pressure.sh hook directly with fixed stdin JSON containing a stable session_id; submit four iterations and assert warnings emit exactly the first 3 times and silently exit on the 4th. Verify both single-mount and both-mounts cases by varying the shim's output' — same session_id across all 4 iterations means scenarios share counter state, and after counter saturates QA cannot distinguish 'rate limit working' from 'shim variation not applied'.
objections.problem: QA running the validation either (a) cannot intercept df/du if the hook absolute-paths them, or (b) gets false negatives because counter is saturated when varying shim output across scenarios.
objections.required_evidence: Add to M2 constraints: 'The hook MUST resolve df and du via PATH (no absolute /bin/df) or accept DF_BIN/DU_BIN environment overrides for test-injection.' Add to context.json validation_approach: 'QA MUST either use a unique session_id per scenario OR delete /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-<token> and /tmp/claude-pressure-warn-<token>.lock between scenarios to prevent counter saturation from masking shim-output variations.'

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 8 findings — 4 actionable (1 BLOCKER + 3 MAJOR + 1 MAJOR validation under-spec) and 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY: (1) M2 flock-in-subshell example contradicts its own parent-shell short-circuit requirement; (2) F5 only partially applied — Edge Cases lines 294-296 retain stale 'stdout-only — dev choice' wording contradicting Technical Hints lines 555-561; (3) atomic-mkdir lock cleanup mismatch with -type f hook-state sweep; (4) AC6 'summed across all categories' contradicts /tmp-only Technical Hint formula; (5) AC3 PATH-shim validation under-specified. Three observation_only: EXCLUDE_PRUNE carve-out is impossible-to-miss; AC7 robustly enumerates the 5 sweep categories (modulo #3); IEC-i examples 12MiB/512KiB/1.2GiB are valid numfmt output.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: QA promoted all 5 actionable codex findings to QA's own MAJOR/BLOCKER objections (#1-#5). QA was wrong to draft PASS — codex caught 4 internal contradictions that should have been caught by the same QA dimensions QA was running (spec_text_vs_execution_drift on F5 in VERIFY-7, scope_alignment on the implementability of M2/M3). QA verdict flips to FAIL. The 3 observation_only findings are recorded as confirmation that EXCLUDE_PRUNE carve-out documentation, AC7 enumeration, and IEC-i examples are adequately specified.

summary.iteration_outcome: BA-QA iteration 2 FAIL. Iteration-1 objections #1-#6 ARE individually resolved against the new ticket text (VERIFY-1 through VERIFY-6 and VERIFY-8 pass; VERIFY-7 partially fails because F5 was only applied to the Technical Hint side, not the Edge Case side). However, codex adversarial review surfaced 4 new actionable contradictions QA missed in the iteration-2 ticket (M2 flock-leak example, F5 stale wording in Edge Cases, atomic-mkdir vs -type f sweep mismatch, AC6 'all categories' vs /tmp-only Technical Hint) plus 1 validation-method under-spec (AC3 PATH-shim).
summary.next_action: Return spec to BA for revision iteration 3. BA must address all 5 objections (OBJ-1 through OBJ-5). The two structural sources of the failures: (a) F5 was applied to ONE site instead of all 3+ sites that mentioned dry-run output, and (b) the M2/M3/AC6 interfaces have implementation-ambiguity that needs concrete code samples — once BA fixes the M2 flock example, mandates a single lock-mechanism, fixes the AC6 freed-bytes calculation, and tightens AC3 validation, the spec should be implementable.
summary.ba_strengths: Iteration-1 objections #1-#6 (happy-attachments line 86, AC1 vs M1, AC5 DIRECTLY, AC4 dry-run channel Technical Hint side, AC3 both-mounts case, settings.json anchor-based citations) are all individually addressed with citable evidence. The major architectural decisions (per-mount banner layout, recursive -mindepth 2 -type f for /tmp/claude-0 .output query, EXCLUDE_PRUNE shared clause with /tmp/claude-0 carve-out, AC7 explicit enumeration of 5 existing sweep categories, IEC-i numfmt) are sound and survive adversarial review.
summary.ba_weaknesses: Partial-application failure pattern: when an issue requires updating MULTIPLE ticket sections (Edge Cases + Technical Hints + AC text), BA fixed ONE section and left the others stale, producing internal contradictions (F5 partial fix). Implementation-detail ambiguity: M2's two-alternative lock mechanism (flock vs atomic-mkdir) plus the example code that contradicts its own surrounding prose, and AC6's calculation gap relative to AC7's preserved /var/tmp/codex-outputs category, produce concrete implementability gaps that would block dev from reaching a passing QA state. Self-verification gap on QA side: VERIFY-7's F5 spot-check inspected only the Technical Hint and concluded 'correct' — should have grep'd across the entire ticket for 'stdout-only' / 'stdout AND log' to catch the stale Edge Case wording. Process improvement: future BA-validation cycles should grep the full ticket for the canonical phrasing of each codex finding to catch partial applications.
